claim: A ProposedReviewMark proposal shall fail validation unless it carries at least one non-whitespace evidence entry.
why: Structural well-formedness gate, not a substance-quality metric: it asserts only that the attestation artifact EXISTS (at least one non-blank evidence string), never that the review was truthful. This keeps it clear of R-boot-cite-measured (REJECTED, REPLACES by R-agent-conduct-is-rules-not-tests), which was a lexical form-metric that produced a compliance score nobody read. This gate produces no metric and no log; it is a fail-loud validation precondition, the same class of syntactic-only enforcement already blessed by R-requirement-freshness-fields / check_requirement_history_wellformed (which requires non-empty at/summary on History entries — the same 'record must not be blank' principle). The substance of the evidence IS reviewed, but by the RESOLVER at the present moment per R-review-mark-requires-substantive-review (INHERENTLY_PROSE); this change is a structural PRECONDITION of that existing PROSE discipline — it guarantees evidence exists to be reviewed — not a replacement or duplicate. Without it, a ReviewMark can stamp last_reviewed_at with zero evidence, indistinguishable from the cmd/seed-due administrative date backfill whose stale attestations the P0-4 content-refresh wave later had to repair. Forward-looking only: the rule constrains the PROPOSAL, not the graph — existing SETTLED nodes that legitimately carry empty evidence remain valid graph state after this change; the gate bites on the next ReviewMark applied. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestApply_ReviewMark_WithoutEvidenceFails and TestApply_ReviewMark_WhitespaceEvidenceFails in internal/proposal/review_mark_test.go mechanically verify a proposal with reviewed_at/review_after set but empty or whitespace-only evidence is rejected by validation.
